agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster delivery with a premium fee, time-window commitment, eligibility constraints, and refund policy for missed windows. Stakeholders from PM, Operations, Finance, Customer Success, and Legal provided requirements during a product discovery workshop.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery — a sub-domain of the Order Fulfillment bounded context, touching Pricing, Driver Assignment, Customer Tracking, and Refunds.","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Express Delivery","definition":"A premium delivery option promising a 2-hour delivery window from order placement, available to eligible customers for an additional fee."},{"term":"Express Fee","definition":"A $12.99 surcharge applied to orders that select the express delivery option."},{"term":"Express Window","definition":"The 2-hour period from order placement within which the delivery must be completed."},{"term":"Pickup SLA","definition":"The 15-minute window from order placement within which a driver must pick up the express order from the warehouse."},{"term":"Express Eligibility","definition":"Customer must be within 10km of a warehouse to select express delivery."},{"term":"Express Driver","definition":"A driver with a minimum 4-star rating who is eligible to fulfill express orders."},{"term":"Missed Window Refund","definition":"Refund of the express fee (not the base delivery fee) when the express window is not met, subject to exclusions."},{"term":"Driver Cancellation Replacement","definition":"Process of assigning a new driver within 10 minutes when a driver cancels mid-delivery on an express order."},{"term":"Wrong Address Exclusion","definition":"Refund exclusion when the delivery window was missed due to the customer providing an incorrect address."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Express delivery option is only available to customers whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Physical constraint — if the customer is beyond 10km, the 2-hour window is operationally infeasible. Allowing ineligible orders would corrupt the delivery promise.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer with a delivery address >10km from any warehouse, WHEN they attempt to select express delivery, THEN the express option must be unavailable."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"Express orders must be picked up by a driver within 15 minutes of order placement.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Operational policy threshold that may be adjusted as fleet capacity changes. Not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express order placed at time T, WHEN no driver has picked up by T+15min, THEN the system must escalate (exact escalation action TBD — see escalations)."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Only drivers with a rating of 4 stars or above may be assigned to express orders.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Quality policy that could be adjusted based on driver supply. A VP could lower to 3.5 stars in a market with limited drivers.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a driver with a rating below 4.0 stars, WHEN the system attempts to assign them to an express order, THEN the assignment must be rejected."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"The express fee is $12.99.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Pricing policy subject to change by Finance or Product.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer selecting express delivery, WHEN the order total is calculated, THEN $12.99 is added as the express fee line item."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"When an express window is missed, only the express fee ($12.99) is refunded — not the base delivery fee.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Refund policy, changeable by business decision.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express order that missed its 2-hour window, WHEN a refund is processed, THEN the refund amount equals the express fee only."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"No refund is issued if the customer provided an incorrect delivery address and that caused the missed window.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Business policy for fraud/error prevention, adjustable.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express order that missed its window AND the delivery address was flagged as incorrect by the driver, WHEN refund eligibility is evaluated, THEN the refund is denied."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Missed-window refunds are processed within 3 business days.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"SLA for finance operations, adjustable.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an approved missed-window refund, WHEN 3 business days have elapsed, THEN the refund must have been processed."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"If an express driver cancels mid-delivery, a replacement driver must be assigned within 10 minutes. If no replacement is assigned within 10 minutes, the express fee is automatically refunded.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Operational and customer-success policy, adjustable.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN an express delivery where the driver cancels mid-route, WHEN 10 minutes elapse without a replacement driver accepting, THEN the system automatically triggers an express fee refund."},{"id":"BR-9","rule":"Customers can track their express driver's location in real time.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Feature requirement, not a data integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"GIVEN a customer with an active express delivery, WHEN they open tracking, THEN they see the driver's location updating in real time."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer whose delivery address is within 10km of a warehouse","when":"They view delivery options at checkout","then":"The express delivery option is displayed with the $12.99 fee and the 2-hour window prominently shown"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer whose delivery address is more than 10km from any warehouse","when":"They view delivery options at checkout","then":"The express delivery option is hidden or disabled with an explanation"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An express order has been placed","when":"The system assigns a driver","then":"Only drivers with a 4.0+ star rating are eligible for assignment"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An express order has been placed","when":"15 minutes pass without a driver picking up the order","then":"The system escalates per the pickup SLA breach workflow"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An express order is delivered after the 2-hour window AND the customer address was valid","when":"The system evaluates refund eligibility","then":"A refund of $12.99 (express fee only) is automatically queued for processing within 3 business days"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An express order missed the 2-hour window AND the customer provided an incorrect address","when":"The system evaluates refund eligibility","then":"No refund is issued and the customer is notified of the reason"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An express driver cancels mid-delivery","when":"A replacement driver is not assigned within 10 minutes","then":"The express fee is automatically refunded to the customer"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A customer has an active express delivery","when":"They open the order tracking view","then":"They see the driver's real-time GPS location and the remaining time in the 2-hour window"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Express order placed at 11:50 PM — the 2-hour window and 3-business-day refund window span midnight and potentially weekends/holidays.","expected_behavior":"The 2-hour delivery window is clock-time from placement regardless of day boundaries. The 3-business-day refund SLA excludes weekends and holidays per Finance calendar."},{"scenario":"Customer is exactly 10km from the warehouse (boundary condition).","expected_behavior":"Needs clarification: is 10km inclusive (≤10km) or exclusive (<10km)? Recommend inclusive (≤10km eligible)."},{"scenario":"Driver rating drops below 4.0 stars mid-delivery of an express order.","expected_behavior":"The active delivery should not be interrupted. Rating eligibility is checked at assignment time, not continuously during delivery."},{"scenario":"Customer changes address after placing an express order.","expected_behavior":"Undefined in spec. If new address is >10km or address change delays delivery, refund exclusion rules and eligibility need to be re-evaluated."},{"scenario":"Multiple driver cancellations on the same express order (first driver cancels, replacement also cancels).","expected_behavior":"The 10-minute replacement window should reset on each cancellation, but the 2-hour delivery window does not reset. If window is exceeded, missed-window refund applies."},{"scenario":"Driver picks up at minute 14 (within 15 min) but delivery is projected to exceed 2 hours.","expected_behavior":"Pickup SLA is met but delivery window may still be missed. The system should flag the order as at-risk but not treat it as a pickup SLA breach."},{"scenario":"Warehouse has no eligible express drivers (all below 4.0 stars).","expected_behavior":"Express option should be dynamically disabled for customers near that warehouse, or the system should attempt to pull drivers from adjacent zones."},{"scenario":"'Wrong address' determination is disputed by the customer.","expected_behavior":"Undefined dispute resolution process. Who determines if the address was 'wrong'? Driver attestation alone may be insufficient — needs a verification workflow."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"New revenue stream at $12.99 per express order. Refund liability for missed windows creates a cost center that must be monitored. Automatic refunds on driver cancellation add uncontrolled cost exposure if driver cancellation rates are high.","operational":"Requires real-time driver assignment within 15 minutes, 4-star driver pool management, real-time GPS tracking infrastructure, and automated refund processing. The 10-minute replacement SLA during driver cancellation is operationally demanding.","user":"Premium experience with real-time tracking and faster delivery. However, the contradiction between 'guarantee' messaging and 'best effort' legal reality risks customer trust if the language is not carefully calibrated.","risk":"HIGH — The guarantee vs. best-effort contradiction is the primary risk. Marketing a 'guarantee' that Legal says cannot be guaranteed exposes the company to false advertising claims, regulatory scrutiny, and customer trust erosion. Secondary risk: wrong-address determination lacks a clear process, creating dispute escalation volume.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Premium delivery experience but potential confusion/frustration if 'guarantee' language is used but not legally honored. Dispute risk on wrong-address refund denials."},{"group":"Drivers","impact":"4-star minimum creates a two-tier driver system. Express-eligible drivers may face higher pressure. Drivers who cancel express orders may face additional penalties (undefined)."},{"group":"Operations","impact":"New SLAs (15-min pickup, 10-min replacement) require real-time monitoring, alerting, and escalation workflows. Significant operational overhead."},{"group":"Finance","impact":"New refund processing pipeline within 3-business-day SLA. Need to track and forecast refund liability as a cost center."},{"group":"Legal/Compliance","impact":"Directly affected — must approve all customer-facing language. The 'guarantee' framing in product and UI must be reconciled with 'best effort' legal position before launch."},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Will handle disputes around wrong-address refund denials, missed-window complaints, and confusion about the guarantee language. Needs clear scripts and escalation paths."}]},"confidence":{"level":40,"reasoning":"The spec contains a direct, unresolved contradiction between stakeholders (PM/CS say 'guarantee', Legal says 'best effort') that fundamentally changes the product's promise, customer-facing language, refund policy enforceability, and system behavior. Until this is resolved, the domain model cannot be finalized. Additionally, several operational details are undefined (pickup SLA breach action, wrong-address verification, address change policy).","high_confidence_areas":["Express fee amount ($12.99)","10km eligibility radius","4-star driver minimum rating","Refund scope limited to express fee only","3-business-day refund processing window","Real-time tracking requirement","10-minute replacement window on driver cancellation"],"low_confidence_areas":["What happens when the 15-minute pickup SLA is breached (escalation action undefined)","How 'wrong address' is determined and by whom","Whether address changes after order placement are allowed","How the 10km distance is calculated (straight-line vs. road distance)","Whether express availability is dynamic based on driver supply"],"assumptions":["The 2-hour window starts at order placement time, not at pickup time","The 10km radius is measured from warehouse to delivery address, not current driver location","'Real time' tracking means GPS updates at least every 10-30 seconds","Business days for refund processing follow the company's standard business calendar","The 4-star rating threshold uses the driver's current cumulative rating, not a rolling window"]},"escalations":[{"type":"contradiction","description":"CRITICAL: PM and Customer Success describe the 2-hour delivery as a 'guarantee' with automatic refunds, while Legal explicitly states this is a 'best effort' SLA and the refund is a 'goodwill gesture, not legally enforceable.' These positions are directly incompatible. If the product says 'guaranteed,' Legal says we cannot back that claim. If Legal prevails, the refund policy may be discretionary rather than automatic, and all customer-facing language must change. This must be resolved before any implementation begins — it affects UI copy, refund automation logic, acceptance criteria, and regulatory exposure.","affected_stakeholders":["Product Management","Legal","Customer Success","Marketing","Customers"],"options":["A) Adopt Legal's position: label as 'Express Priority' with 'estimated 2-hour delivery' and discretionary goodwill refunds. Safest legally but weakest product positioning.","B) Adopt PM's position: market as 'guaranteed' with automatic refunds, accepting Legal risk. Requires Legal sign-off on specific terms and conditions that limit liability.","C) Hybrid: use 'Express Commitment' language with automatic refunds framed as a 'service credit' rather than a contractual obligation. Requires Legal to draft acceptable terms."],"recommendation":"Option C — a hybrid approach that provides the customer confidence PM and CS want while giving Legal the non-contractual framing they require. Legal must draft the specific language before development begins."},{"type":"contradiction","description":"The automatic refund on missed window (PM, Finance) vs. 'not legally enforceable' refund (Legal) creates ambiguity in system behavior. Should the system automatically issue refunds on missed windows, or should it flag for manual review? Automatic refunds align with PM/CS intent but contradict Legal's 'goodwill gesture' framing which implies discretion.","affected_stakeholders":["Finance","Legal","Engineering","Customer Support"],"options":["A) Fully automatic refunds on missed windows (PM/CS position) — system issues refund with no human review.","B) Semi-automatic — system flags missed windows and queues refunds for approval by CS team before processing.","C) Fully automatic but with a 'goodwill credit' label in the system and customer communications, not 'refund' — satisfying Legal's framing while maintaining automation."],"recommendation":"Option C — automate the credit but label and terms must reflect goodwill, not obligation. This preserves operational efficiency while respecting Legal's position."}],"rejection_reasons":[]}
agent_output_excerpt: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"New 'Express Delivery' product offering faster delivery with a premium fee, time-window commitment, eligibility constraints, and refund policy for missed windows. Stakeholders from PM, Operations, Finance, Customer Success, and Legal provided requirements during a product discovery workshop.","bounded_context":"Express Delivery — a sub-domain of the Order Fulfillment bounded context, touching Pricing, Driver Assignment, Customer Tracking, and Refunds.","ub

content.thinking:
Let me analyze these workshop notes carefully for contradictions and gaps.

Key contradiction: PM and Customer Success say "guaranteed within 2 hours" but Legal says it's "best effort SLA, not a contractual guarantee." This is a direct contradiction about the nature of the commitment.

Let me also look for other issues...

PM says "guaranteed" + refund if missed. Legal says "best effort" + refund is "goodwill gesture." These directly conflict on the nature of the promise.

This is a contradiction scenario. All escalations should be type "contradiction". Confidence ≤ 50.


Even though there's disagreement on the core commitment, I can still extract the business rules and acceptance criteria from the parts where there's alignment—like the 2-hour timeframe itself and the refund mechanism, even if the legal nature of those commitments is disputed.
